summary: McKenzie notes that the Antair team, fellow micro-ISV entrepreneurs he has followed, are moving up to bigger operations with new offices. He reflects on how Andrey from Antair used to benchmark his sales against McKenzie's, but has since surpassed him significantly. The brief post serves as a collegial shout-out within the small software business community.
key_points: Antair has grown from a micro-ISV to requiring real office space, signaling meaningful business growth
McKenzie reflects on the early days when peers benchmarked against each other's modest sales numbers
The micro-ISV community was tight-knit enough that founders tracked each other's progress openly
money_quotes: I remember back when Andrey was still comparing his sales to mine to see if he was on a good track.
Suffice it to say that he's since made it necessary for me to play a wee bit of catchup.
key_insight: In small software entrepreneurship communities, founders openly benchmark against peers, and watching colleagues outgrow you is both humbling and motivating.
